rows.rationale:
OI-055 (frontier/B771_phase1_wave1/cells/OI-055/{compute.py,output.txt}) formalizes one premise of B685's terminal no-go (frontier/B685_generation_terminal/FINDINGS.md): the figure-eight's Habiro/Nahm series Ψ=Φ(h)Φ(−h) cannot supply the 5-adic ({2/5,3/5} "hearing") denominators needed by the flavor-stream carriers, because it is claimed integral away from 3.

WHAT IS GENUINELY PROVEN (all-n, machine-checked, correct on independent re-derivation): (1) 5 is inert in ℤ[ω] — Legendre(−3,5)=−1, T²+T+1 irreducible mod 5, verified directly; (2) any c∈ℤ[ω][1/3] has v₅(c)≥0 via the norm form a²−ab+b² (elementary, correct); (3) the "hearing" side is a REAL theorem, not a numeric bound: B683's divided-power law v₅(c_n)=−(n+v₅(n!)) for (q;q)_∞^{−3/5} is proved all-n by a strict 5-adic ultrametric-dominance argument (diagonal term uniquely minimal valuation) — I re-derived the argument and it is sound. So the LOGICAL SCAFFOLD ("if 3-integral, then no order can carry 5-adic hearing denominators") is genuinely STRUCTURAL and robust.

WHAT IS NOT CLOSED: the load-bearing premise — that Ψ is 3-integral at EVERY order — is not proved in-sandbox. It is verified to n=20 in-cell via two independent routes (Kashaev-sum PSLQ extraction on disjoint ladders here; an independent saddle-point/holomorphic-blocks derivation in the B742/B739 recompute, both matching GSWZ's own printed eq.(1)/(2) coefficients exactly) and cross-checked against GSWZ's own published n=100 exponent (3¹⁴⁶, independently confirmed as a real transcribed fact in B682's PHASE0_EXTRACTION_CC.md). A perturbation test (r₃→r₃+ε) shows the property is NOT a free consequence of the symmetrization structure — genuinely nontrivial, guarding against the "obviously true, checked trivially" failure mode. But closure to "all n" rests entirely on citing GSWZ arXiv:2412.04241 by title/general construction, without pinning a specific theorem/proposition number establishing Habiro-ring membership FOR THIS SPECIFIC SERIES (contrast: the companion Gevrey/factorial fact about the unsymmetrized Φ(h) IS pinned to "Thm 9.1" in B682's adjudication; the symmetrized 3-integrality claim carries no such pin anywhere in the repo). This is exactly the B525-lesson risk (necessary evidence to depth 100 read as sufficient for "all n") — mitigated by honest self-labeling (RESOLVED-B "OBSTRUCTED," not RESOLVED-A/CLOSED-theorem) but not eliminated. Axis 2 = MARGINAL.

AXIS 3 (level/projection): not applicable in the B766 trace/chord sense — this is an arithmetic-integrality computation on a Habiro/Kashaev q-series, not an SL(n) character-variety trace-map computation with a theta-odd chord sector it could be blind to; no parallel chord-level target is at risk of being missed here.

AXIS 4 (input mechanism) — the most adversarial finding: B685's FINDINGS explicitly reads this result as confirming the program's OWN pre-existing seam thesis ("the object is being; the hearing values live in the coupling, not the object"), and self-labels that reading "[Firewalled reading; cites the memory, not CLAIMS.]" The "hearing" target ({2/5,3/5} 5-adic flavor-stream structure) is named and motivated as a stand-in for generation/flavor structure — an IMPORTED-flavored target by the program's own vocabulary — even though it is operationalized as an object-native q-series ((q;q)_∞^{−2/5,−3/5}), so input_class is genuinely MIXED rather than clean object-native. Given the thesis already predicts no object-native mechanism produces observer-coupling values, a negative eliminating one more candidate mechanism (Habiro/Nahm, after the earlier Modular/Γ(5) kill) is real incremental content (Route B's golden/(2,5) system was a genuine candidate that looked live until killed by a geometric inconsistency — this wasn't a foregone conclusion) but its ultimate narrative payload duplicates B706 rather than standing as independent new evidence; hence independent_of_seam=false.

OVERALL: PROVISIONAL, not ROBUST and not CONTENT-FREE. The math that IS proved (inertness, the norm-form lemma, the all-n divided-power theorem, the nontriviality/perturbation test, cross-ladder + cross-source depth-20/100 agreement) is solid and adds real, falsifiable content beyond the seam thesis. But the negative's closing step depends on an external theorem cited by title only (no pinned statement/number for the specific membership claim), verified only to finite (if substantial) depth, and the interpretive payload is explicitly self-flagged as echoing B706. Recompute recommended: (a) locate and pin the exact GSWZ theorem/proposition establishing Habiro-ring-of-ℚ(√−3) membership for Φ(h)Φ(−h) specifically (or for the general class of hyperbolic-WRT symmetrized series it belongs to), the way Thm 9.1 was pinned for the Gevrey fact; (b) if no such all-order statement exists in the source (only a worked example to n=100), downgrade the census label from "RESOLVED-B → EXTERNAL" toward "RESOLVED-B → SEARCH-BOUNDED (depth 100)" rather than treating the citation as a closing theorem.

CORE FINITE COMPUTATION IS SOLID: [Γ_bar:Γ_bar∩gΓ_barg⁻¹]=10 at level 8 is exact modular arithmetic over Z[ω]/8, cross-checked via 2 ring-basis implementations (omega and B734's z-basis) and, more importantly, an algorithmically-distinct explicit 12-coset orbit-stabilizer enumeration (orbit_size=10) — genuinely independent code paths, no floating point involved, no bugs found. The g⁻¹-direction and level-16 checks add confirmation but are logically near-implied once the main claim holds (weaker independence than advertised, but not wrong).

CRITICAL DEFECT FOUND (adversarial, in-sandbox reproduced): PART 1a, the cell's own "independent, M-free" certificate that M=8 is PROVABLY an exact congruence level ("decisive already at M=8... no B731-style premature-plateau risk") is BROKEN in two compounding ways. (1) It reuses a KNOWN-BUGGY mpmath nsum computation: I reran humbert_true_index_check() and independently confirmed L(2,χ₋₃) via naive nsum returns 0.7725 (should be ≈0.7813, verified by manual 300k-term partial sum) and vol(m004) via the sin-series returns 2.007013873270436563874928448219929605195 — NOT the true figure-eight-complement volume 2.029883212819307 (SnapPy, ~1.1% off). This exact buggy pair of numbers is already sitting, unfixed, in frontier/B734_m004_is_congruence/cc3_verification/results.json, and that same directory ALSO contains the fix (b734_fix2.py, docstring: "Humbert via Hurwitz zeta (exact-precision L) against the INDEPENDENT snappy volume (non-vacuous)"), which OI-146 did not use. (2) Worse: I proved algebraically that vol_m004/vol_orbifold = 12 EXACTLY by construction regardless of the (buggy or correct) numeric value of the shared "lob" term (zeta(2)=π²/6 and 3^1.5/√3=3 cancel exactly), so PART 1a's "ratio rounds to 12: True" is an UNFALSIFIABLE TAUTOLOGY — it cannot ever output anything but 12, so it certifies nothing. This is a real MB12-style vacuity violation dressed as decisive independent evidence.

DOES THE FINAL VERDICT SURVIVE? Likely yes, but not because of this cell. The needed background fact (true geometric index=12, and per B734's cc3 ADDENDUM the STANDARD congruence level is already (4), so a fortiori (8)) is correctly, validly established elsewhere in-repo via the Hurwitz-zeta + actual-SnapPy computation (cc3_verification/b734_fix2.py, ADDENDUM_CC3_2026-07-21.md: ratio 11.9999999999), and OI-146's background section does cite that lineage (B302/B734/cc3_verification) for the "known GEOMETRIC index 12" fact. So the correspondence-theorem argument (Γ(8) normal in ambient ⟹ conjugation-invariant ⟹ finite-quotient computation = true index) is mechanically sound GIVEN that background fact — OI-146 just failed to actually re-derive it in-cell as claimed, borrowing a broken formula that coincidentally cannot help but agree.

AXIS CLASSIFICATION: KIND=MIXED (a genuinely exact/structural finite-group computation, whose own resolution-adequacy certificate is an invalid quasi-empirical/vacuous check rather than a proof). RESOLUTION=MARGINAL (the in-cell "no premature plateau" argument is unearned; the true supporting fact exists but only outside this cell, uncredited as the actual proof). LEVEL/PROJECTION=N/A — this is a Bianchi-group/hyperbolic-arithmetic commensurator-index computation using full matrix/subgroup structure (not reduced to traces), unrelated to the theta-odd/chord-vs-trace framework; B766-style trace-blindness does not apply here. INPUT=OBJECT-NATIVE and INDEPENDENT of the B706 seam — Γ=π₁(4₁) via the Riley discrete-faithful rep, g the B302 order-3 commensurator element, and the commensurator index itself are all intrinsic arithmetic-topology structure of the object, not an imported SM/observer-side target (no Koide/θW/mass-ratio numerology here), so this negative is not thesis-predicted-content-free.

VERDICT: PROVISIONAL, not ROBUST. The headline (claimed index 3 refuted, true value 10) is probably correct, but the cell oversold its own rigor with a broken, unfalsifiable certifying sub-computation for the single most load-bearing premise (no premature plateau). RECOMPUTE recommended: rerun PART 1a using the Hurwitz-zeta closed form L=(ζ(2,1/3)-ζ(2,2/3))/9 and a real SnapPy volume call (both already implemented correctly in frontier/B734_m004_is_congruence/cc3_verification/b734_fix2.py) so the "decisive at M=8" claim is actually earned in-cell rather than borrowed-by-luck through a tautology. Files: frontier/B771_phase1_wave1/cells/OI-146/{compute.py,output.txt,output.json}; contrast frontier/B734_m004_is_congruence/cc3_verification/{b734_fix2.py,ADDENDUM_CC3_2026-07-21.md,results.json}.

OI-173 (frontier/B771_phase1_wave1/cells/OI-173/compute.py, output.txt) refutes H103's hoped 'clean theorem' (every 2T-in-E6 embedding is sigma-stable, so the hierarchy is stuck at Level 4) by an EXPLICIT COUNTEREXAMPLE: the center-twisted trinification rho'(g)=w^{eps(g)}.rho_b(g), where eps:2T->Z/3 is the exact abelianization homomorphism (verified over all 576 pairs, exact) and w is the E6 center mu_3 scalar (w^3=1, exact). This is a universal claim killed by one witness -- logically the strongest possible negative, not a search-absence claim.

AXIS 1 (KIND): STRUCTURAL, not search-bounded. 2T is a fixed finite group (order 24, cross-checked against the known SL(2,3) character table: 7 classes sizes 1,1,4,4,6,4,4, dims 1,1,1,2,2,2,3, sum-sq=24 -- matches). The homomorphism property of rho' follows algebraically from three independently-proved exact facts (eps hom, rho3=1'+2' hom via a block Cayley-style construction, w^3=1) -- I traced the block-diagonal kron construction by hand and confirmed Pb(g)=P_of(rho3(g),I,I) is provably multiplicative given rho3 is; the numeric 1e-10 check (all 576 pairs) is a redundant confirmation, not the load-bearing evidence. Invariance of the cubic I3 under both 2T generators is checked SYMBOLICALLY (sympy, exact), and since Pt is an exact homomorphism, invariance propagates to the whole group by pure algebra (correctly noted in-cell). The one numeric component (Part F: dim Stab(I3)=78=dim e6, via random-sample SVD nullity, 2 seeds) is a side sanity-check of a textbook fact (Stab of the nondegenerate Freudenthal cubic on C^27 is E6), not the discriminating fact, and is extremely well-conditioned (SVD gap ~1e14) -- not a marginal bound.

AXIS 2 (RESOLUTION): not applicable in the search-bounded sense -- this is an exact algebraic existence proof, so the usual 'could the effect live just beyond the swept bound' question doesn't arise the way it does for a numeric sweep.

AXIS 3 (LEVEL/PROJECTION): the discriminating fact (D6/D7) IS a trace/character computation, but for finite-group complex representations in characteristic 0, character is a COMPLETE isomorphism invariant (two reps are isomorphic iff their characters agree) -- there is no chord/word-level information a character could hide here, unlike the hyperbolic trace-map contexts where cyclic-word traces lose ordering data. So this is trace-level but adequate, not blind-and-risky.

AXIS 4 (INPUT MECHANISM): fully OBJECT-NATIVE. 2T is the McKay/binary-tetrahedral finite subgroup, E6 is Stab of the object's own cubic invariant (trinification/27), w is tied to the object's own arithmetic atom Q(sqrt(-3)) (Eisenstein omega, already the B356 chirality-window mechanism). No Koide/mixing-angle/coupling-constant/mass-ratio ever enters; the cell's own header states 'Firewalled: structural only, no SM values, nothing to CLAIMS' and this is accurate on inspection. This is independent of the B706 seam thesis (values-in-coupling-not-object) -- it is a new McKay/E6 representation-theoretic fact about which rung of an internal 'Level' hierarchy is populated, not a restatement of the seam in different language.

ADVERSARIAL CHECK (the one real subtlety, and it survives): Part E honestly flags that the abstract SUBGROUP H'=Im(rho') might be E6-conjugate to its complex-conjugate image via precomposition with 2T's outer automorphism alpha (verified exactly: chi_tw(alpha(g))=conj(chi_tw(g))). This raises whether 'sigma-stable' should be tested at the subgroup level (where this witness might not actually be new) rather than the fixed-embedding/character level. I checked this against B329's own operational definition (mckay_embeddings.py: n1 vs n2 computed on a FIXED embedding's 27-restriction, exactly the same recipe used here) -- the sealed criterion is faithful to the program's own prior usage, not a redefinition to force RESOLVED-B. The cell also shows the conclusion is labeling-robust: {n1,n2}={9,0} as a SET is invariant under the alpha-relabeling, so 'n1!=n2' survives regardless of which order-3 class is called 'class 1' vs 'class 2'. I additionally confirmed independently that chi_twisted cannot be a relabeling of the real-valued chi_balanced (real vs genuinely complex values at the same class-order structure), so this is a genuinely new, non-B329 embedding, not a disguised repeat.

Overall this is a well-audited, exact, self-critical, object-native structural refutation with no smuggled SM content and no trace-level blind spot for the specific claim it tests. Verdict: ROBUST.

Read frontier/B771_phase1_wave1/cells/OI-186/compute.py + output.txt, the PREREGISTRATION.md sealed criterion, FINDINGS.md's banked line, the wave1_results.json machine record (discriminating_fact/verify_note/verify_issues), and the originating HINT_LEDGER.md H112 entry (which literally instructs "compute the family table before believing anything" — this cell does exactly that).

Independently re-derived by hand: tr(A1)=3, tr(A2)=6, A1A2 trace=15 (matrix product checked entry-by-entry), the closed forms tr(A_mA_n)=(mn)²+(m+n)²+2 and tr(A_mA_n⁻¹)=(m-n)²+2, the conductor arithmetic for seam(1)=15 and seam(2)=8/16 (fund_disc and lcm computed by hand, matches script), and the Pell-unit-orbit claim (u²-6k²=19, orbit under 5+2√6: (5,1)→(37,15)→(365,149), verified exactly). All checked out.

AXIS 1 (KIND) — MIXED but with a STRUCTURAL core. The decisive kill (T1: "is seam(2)=8 or 16 attainable by tr(A_mA_n)?") is an exact finite Diophantine-impossibility proof, not a bounded sweep: since (mn)²≤14 forces mn≤3, the check enumerates a genuinely finite, complete set of candidates and none hits 6 or 14. That is a real theorem-shaped negative, not "swept to N and found nothing." The auxiliary generalization sweeps (T2 scale-scale, T3 Eisenstein-anchored, T4 cross-pairings) are search-bounded (m,n≤30/60/300, k≤400), but they are reinforced by an exact mechanism (T5/S5: the recurring 15-hits are proven to be a Pell-unit orbit of u²-6k²=19, propagating forever under multiplication by 5+2√6, plus an anchor-swap control at c=7,11,13) — this converts "no hits found in range" into "hits exist but are provably generic conic/Pell arithmetic with no law content," which is strictly stronger evidence than a bare negative sweep.

AXIS 2 (RESOLUTION) — ADEQUATE. The one genuinely numeric/bounded sub-claim is that the geometry (shape) field is non-quadratic for m=3,4 (algdep degree-scan to 8, height filter <1e8, cross-checked at 30 and 60 dps, both precisions agreeing, and matching the prior citation B125/Bowditch–Maclachlan–Reid arithmeticity finiteness). The cell's own verifier (recorded in wave1_results.json) flagged precisely this as the one soft spot — "generalizes from only 2 in-cell data points" — and correctly noted the verdict does NOT depend on it, since the m=2 exact-impossibility fact alone already forces RESOLVED-B. I confirm that reasoning: even granting maximal uncertainty about m≥3, the law is already dead at its only defined second test point.

AXIS 3 (LEVEL/PROJECTION) — N/A. This negative is not a trace-vs-chord (theta-odd-sector) question. Both sides being compared — tr(A_mA_n) and the biquadratic conductor of two number fields intrinsic to the metallic/geometric data — are genuinely trace/field invariants on both the hypothesis and the test; there is no order-sensitive "chord-level" content being tested here or plausibly hidden by cyclic-trace symmetry. The trace-blindness concern (B766-style) doesn't bite this cell.

AXIS 4 (INPUT MECHANISM) — OBJECT-NATIVE, independent of the seam thesis. Both quantities are object-generated: tr(A_mA_n) is a trace in the program's own metallic-generator trace map; the "seam" conductor is built from the object's own scale field ℚ(√(m²+4)) and its own SnapPy-computed geometry (shape) field of the punctured-torus bundle — no SM/observer parameter (Koide, sin²θW, mass ratios) enters anywhere (verify_issues confirms Gate 5/5-Q clean, no SM terms). Because the input is fully internal, this negative is not automatically predicted by the B706 "values live in observer coupling" seam thesis — it is a genuine test of whether two independently-arising object invariants coincide by law or by numerology, and it could have gone the other way. It did not: the result is a disciplined refutation of a specific coincidence (the "two 15s"), with the coincidence itself explained mechanistically (Pell-unit orbits) rather than merely declared absent.

Minor scope note (does not change the verdict): H112's "seam(m)" generalizes H122's canonical fixed seam √-15 (golden end × Eisenstein end of the whole object) into a per-metallic-member family; the cell is faithful to H112 as preregistered and literally written, but a reader should note "the seam" in H122 is a single object-level constant, and this cell tests a natural but broader per-m extension of it — the cell itself surfaces this via T4's "mismatched index structure" finding (trace-15 is a two-member invariant, seam-15 a one-member invariant), which if anything strengthens the independence conclusion rather than weakening it.

Overall: no NECESSARY-read-as-SUFFICIENT error (the B525 lesson) — the exact impossibility check is genuinely sufficient for the literal H112 claim; no thesis-recapitulation-in-costume (axis 4 clean); no hidden trace-blind gap (axis 3 N/A); the one search-bounded piece is adequately cross-validated and explicitly shown not to be load-bearing for the verdict. This is one of the more carefully self-audited negatives in the wave — recompute not needed.

Reproduced compute.py myself (frontier/B771_phase1_wave1/cells/W2-148r/compute.py, 0.3s, 22/22 gates PASS, output.json byte-identical to banked artifact) and cross-read t1_continuum.py, PREREG_WAVE2.md, FINDINGS_WAVE2.md, wave2_results.json, and the locked test (tests/test_b771_wave1.py: W2-148r in the upheld set).

AXIS 1 (kind) — STRUCTURAL, not search-bounded. The claim (|12L(chi)|^2 = 9, not the registered 27, at level 27) is an exact closed-form identity 12L = 3*zeta_{3^k}^{-1} proved symbolically in Q[z]/Phi_27, and confirmed by two independent exact routes: (a) F_p arithmetic on the raw B399 20-prime residue data (12L*conj(12L) ≡ 9 mod every one of 20 large primes, with the SAME phase t=17 at all 20), and (b) mpmath at 50/100-digit precision. No PSLQ/height sweep anywhere (verify_note explicitly checks this). The one search-bounded piece — Step 6's freeze-lemma extension to rungs k=2..7 — is correctly hedged in both the printed log and output.json as "conditional on the orbit persisting; data-proven at k=2,3" and is NOT used in the verdict, which rests only on k=2 (anchor) and k=3 (target), both data-confirmed.

AXIS 2 (resolution) — N/A for the core claim (exact, not swept). The 20-prime corroboration is unanimous with zero exceptions, more than adequate as a check on the symbolic proof rather than the sole evidence.

AXIS 3 (level/projection) — the specific "trace-map blind to chord/theta-odd sector" concern (B766) belongs to a different sub-program (SL(n,C) character-variety trace maps of the figure-eight object); this arc's math is Galois/cyclotomic (a tower measure's character resolvents over Q(zeta_{3^k})), not that machinery, so I mark N/A rather than force a mapping. That said, the analogous general discipline is honored: the cell does NOT stop at the symmetric/mean invariant L(chi_0) (=0, uninformative) but resolves into the individual nontrivial-character projections L(chi_1), L(chi_2) and computes both exactly — i.e., it goes past the coarsest statistic before concluding. (Minor note: since the innovation values v_c are real, L(chi_2)=conj(L(chi_1)) is automatic, so "both pairings agree" is expected, not a second independent check — doesn't affect correctness, just tempers how much extra evidence that particular gate adds.)

AXIS 4 (input mechanism) — OBJECT-NATIVE. Every quantity is internally generated: raw prime-indexed tower residues (B399), roots of unity/minimal polynomials/the unique quadratic subfield of Q(zeta27), Galois trace. Nothing is an imported SM/observer-side target (no Koide, no mixing angle, no mass ratio); Gate 5/5-Q explicitly confirms no SM vocabulary and nothing touched CLAIMS.md. The negative is therefore independent of the B706 seam critique, not a re-costumed repetition of it.

Adversarial checks I ran/verified beyond the artifact's own verify_note: confirmed t1_continuum.py's "registered prediction" really is a bare `return 3**k` with a self-admittedly uncertain orbit_m=[1,4,7] guess ("an order-3 orbit under m -> m+3? use the Frobenius...") and an un-normalized (no /12) numeric check — so the object being refuted really was weakly specified, and the audit cell's reconstruction of the true level-27 object from the actual banked B399 residues (not a guess) is the more principled comparison, not a moved goalpost. Confirmed the level-9/level-27 comparison is a genuine discriminant: both hypotheses (frozen-at-9 vs growing-3^k) agree at the anchor (9=3^2 either way) and are separated for the first time at k=3, which is exactly where the computation is targeted. An independent prior verifier (recorded in wave2_results.json) ran 8 targeted refutation attempts (fresh-code reruns at unprivileged prime indices, independent minpoly sweep over N in {1,2,3,4,6,12,24} confirming 12 is the minimal integral clearing, grep-confirmed the banked B413 source of "12" so it isn't refitted) and found no defect. I found no additional weakness. Overall: a genuine, well-corroborated, appropriately-hedged structural negative on an object-native target — ROBUST.

Search-bounded PSLQ negative on a genuinely object-native quantity (per-letter hyperbolic volume of the Fibonacci punctured-torus-bundle tower), correctly self-labeled RESOLVED-B (not oversold as a transcendence proof). Resolution is adequate: 126 trusted digits reached via independently cross-validated volumes (own Bloch-Wigner vs SnapPy's quad-double, plus 384-bit and mirror-word conditioning seeds), algebraicity swept to degree 8/height 1e10, six candidate-basis sweeps (singles/pairs/triples/full-basis/golden-weighted) at heights scaled correctly to the tolerance-height house rule. The classic internal-relation/circularity trap (W2-148-style) was caught and independently residual-verified rather than mis-flagged. A second agent reran the ENTIRE pipeline from scratch (deleted volumes.json, regenerated all shapes) and reproduced byte-identical output, ruling out fabrication/citation. The cell is explicit about what remains open (a Guéritaud angle-equation/ergodic-average evaluation, not more PSLQ height) rather than falsely claiming exhaustion. Axis 3 (trace/chord level) doesn't bear on this arc — it's a direct geometric volume invariant, not a trace-projection of the theta-odd SL(n) program, so no blind-spot risk of that kind applies. Axis 4: c is the object's own volume-growth constant, not an imported SM/observer target, so the negative is independent of the B706 seam and not thesis-predicted-content-free. Two purely cosmetic labeling slips (28-vs-30-digit banked string, "13" vs actual 12-element post-drop basis) don't affect the verdict. This is as sound as a bounded PSLQ negative gets in this repo; no basis found to downgrade to PROVISIONAL or flag for recompute.

W2-140/B157 asks whether the metallic peripheral exponent k in [A,B]=s·µ^k (µ=A⁻ᵐt, on the SL(n) figure-eight trace-map character variety) has a closed form in (m,o,n). Read the full lineage (B154→B157→B198→B199→W2-140): this is NOT a single fresh negative, it is the 4th pass at the same question, and it correctly inherits B199's genuine structural results — two PROVEN collisions (A²(o=4)=A³(o=6)=diag(1,-1,-1) exactly, yet k=2 vs k=1; the o=4 column is non-monotone in m) that rigorously kill any k(o,m)-only or k(A^m-spectrum)-only law. Those pieces are STRUCTURAL. W2-140's own contribution is narrower: propose k=f(e,g) (e=proj. order of A^m, g=gcd(m,eff)) as the surviving invariant pair, test 7 named candidate closed forms (C1-C7) plus a brute-force 1-2-feature affine search, using genuine predict-before-compute discriminator cells (P1-P5, fixed before computing) — good practice, not post-hoc curve-fitting. Result: every candidate fails; "no closed law found" is explicitly self-labeled bounded ("table banks... bounded, not forced"), not claimed as proof — correct epistemic hygiene (satisfies GOVERNANCE §6.1(C)).

AXIS 1 (KIND) = MIXED: genuine proofs embedded (B199) + an open-ended bounded search (W2-140's own (e,g) candidates) for the general law.

AXIS 2 (RESOLUTION) = MARGINAL, adversarially checked. C7 = floor((8-e-g)/g) fits 13 of the 14 banked points and fails at exactly ONE point: (m=1,o=6,n=6), established k=4 vs C7's predicted k=1. That single point is the entire hinge between "no closed law" and "closed law found" — and in the banked artifact it rested on only 3 total probe hits (1/5 + 2/8 across 2 RNG streams). I independently re-ran probe_k.py on this exact cell with fresh/extended seeds (targeted k=1 vs k=4, s=+1): k=1 → 0/30 hits (both streams); k=4 → 7/30 hits, with excellent numerics on the new hits (cond(t)=147 and 15.6, residuals 8.8e-13 and 7.6e-14, genuine mixed-magnitude loxodromic µ-spectra) — this REINFORCES the banked k=4 value rather than overturning it, so the crux data point held up under adversarial re-probing. However the search itself stays narrow: only 7 hand-picked forms + a tiny brute-force space (≤2 features, integer coefficients in [-4,4], offset in [-8,8]) were tried; and the data itself shows the tested invariant pair (e,g) is incomplete — at the SAME (e,g)=(6,1), m1_o6_n4 (n=4) is sublocus-EMPTY while m1_o6_n6 (n=6) gives k=4, i.e. rank n governs EXISTENCE and isn't jointly combined with (e,g) in any tested candidate. So the negative is well-verified on the data side but the closed-form search space is too narrow to license "no law" beyond the specific forms tried.

AXIS 3 (LEVEL) = chord-level, not trace-blind: k is read via a full n×n matrix identity comm=s·µ^k (exponent() in gauge_newton.py), computed on explicit gauge-fixed Newton-solved representations, correctly restricted to the order(µ)=∞ rigid sublocus per B199 Result B's own (verify-don't-trust) correction — this is stronger than a trace-coordinate computation and is not vulnerable to the B766 trace-blindness failure mode.

AXIS 4 (INPUT) = OBJECT-NATIVE and independent of the B706 seam: (m,o,n,exps) are internal trace-map/character-variety parameters (metallic slope index, boundary-matrix cyclic order, rank), not an imported SM/coupling target — this negative is not thesis-predicted or content-free.

Net: PROVISIONAL, not ROBUST. WHAT IT ACTUALLY IS: house review already caught a real defect. FINDINGS_WAVE3.md lists W3-067 as REFUSED AT GATE ('math-sound, framing overclaim' -- calling 5 distinct deterministic words '5 independent seeds' misused house statistical-seed language). The carry-fix W4-067r reframes the same computation as '6 distinct words under one fixed method,' adds a 6th word, and re-derives from scratch (all_checks_pass=true, verdict RESOLVED-B) -- but this carry is currently UNTRACKED/uncommitted in git status, i.e. it has not itself passed a wave-4 gate review yet. So the 'banked negative' handed to this audit is, strictly, still mid-repair.

AXIS 1 (KIND) -- SEARCH-BOUNDED, not structural. The compositum-containment test (Re(z),Im(z) in Q(sqrt3)) is exact algebra, but the actual detection mechanism is mpmath PSLQ at fixed height (<=1e8) and precision (~63-70 digits, tol=1e-49) -- a numerical null result over a finite, hand-picked sample of 5-6 composite words, not a certified symbolic/Galois-theoretic proof that no composite word's trace field can ever fuse. No exact Sage/Groebner trace-field certification was run as a cross-check.

AXIS 2 (RESOLUTION) -- MARGINAL. Genuinely good scout hygiene: positive controls correctly reproduce the KNOWN golden/silver seed fields from scratch, two independent vacuity self-tests (free transcendental, unrelated quadratic) both correctly fail, and the negative is reproduced at an independently truncated ~40-digit precision (conditioning). But the universal claim ('classical composition does NOT fuse') is inferred from only 5-6 explicitly chosen words out of an infinite word space -- adequate for a scout-grade breadth check, not for a closed theorem, and the cell's own docstring frames the outcome as pre-expected ('SnapPy-gated, expected negative per K016' -- stated in B193 *before* this cell ran), which weakens how much new discriminating information the negative actually carries versus confirming a genericity prior (arithmetic hyperbolic 3-manifolds are known-sparse; identify() on all 5-6 composite manifolds returns non-census names, consistent with the expected generic/non-arithmetic outcome).

AXIS 3 (LEVEL) -- TRACE-BLIND-RISK, the sharpest adversarial finding. The classical side is computed strictly at the INVARIANT TRACE FIELD level (tr(a^2), tr(b^2), tr((ab)^2) -- the Neumann-Reid-complete generating set for a 2-generator Kleinian group, correctly chosen and correctly validated against known K015/B125/B129 baselines). But the quantum comparison target (B132's SU(2)_k eigenvalue field reaching Q(zeta12) via the mod-4 T-matrix twist) is NOT a classical-limit of the invariant trace field in any proven sense -- it is a Reshetikhin-Turaev/quantum-group construction built from FRAMING and Dehn-twist (chord/curve-specific) data at a root of unity, a genuinely different mathematical object from the group's abstract trace field. The cell treats 'does the classical invariant trace field fuse' as the obvious classical analog of the quantum fusion mechanism without establishing that analogy is complete. A classical shadow of the mod-4 twist could in principle live in framing/curve-specific (chord-level, non-conjugacy-invariant) data that the abstract invariant-trace-field computation is blind to by construction. This mirrors exactly the program's own B766 theta-T6 lesson (traces are cyclic-invariant and blind to non-trace/chord structure) and was not addressed anywhere in the cell or its carry.

AXIS 4 (INPUT MECHANISM) -- OBJECT-NATIVE, passes cleanly. Both sides of the comparison (the metallic-tower manifolds' own invariant trace fields, and the program's own B132 SU(2)_k eigenvalue construction) are internally generated by the object itself; nothing here is an imported observer-side target (no Koide/sin^2thetaW/mass-ratio substitution). So this negative is independent of the B706 c/theta seam and is not thesis-predicted content-free by that route.

OVERALL -- PROVISIONAL, not ROBUST. The arithmetic is competently executed with real controls (better than a typical numerology-risk PSLQ scout), and it is not a seam-costume repeat -- but it fails to clear the bar for a closed structural negative on three independent grounds: (1) it is a small-sample numerical scout, not a proof, generalized to a universal claim; (2) the in-house gate itself already refused the wave-3 version once for a real (if secondary) defect, and its fix is not yet re-gated/committed; (3) the classical-vs-quantum comparison is made at the trace level while the quantum side's actual mechanism is framing/chord-sensitive, leaving a live, uncontrolled risk that a genuine classical fusion analog exists one level down that this negative cannot see. Recompute recommended: (a) push W4-067r through an actual wave-4 gate/commit rather than leaving it as the de facto answer while still untracked; (b) test whether framing/twist-sensitive classical data (traces of the specific Dehn-twist curves entering the R/L word, not just the abstract group's invariant trace field) fuses, before calling 'classical does not mimic quantum' closed; (c) if resources allow, replace/augment the PSLQ bounded-height search with an exact symbolic (Sage Groebner/number-field) trace-field certification on at least the 2-block cases to convert this from search-bounded to structural.

The raw arithmetic is exact and quadruply cross-verified (in-cell form enumeration sanity-tested against 13 textbook class numbers, Redei-Reichardt Legendre-symbol criterion, Dirichlet analytic class-number sum, and external PARI/GP at verify) with a genuine non-vacuity control (3,13) that flips sign -- h(-8899)=14, Sylow_2(Cl(-8899))=Z/2 exactly, forced-cyclic by unconditional genus theory (2-rank=t-1=1 for t=2 prime-discriminant factors), so 4-rank=0 is a COMPLETE answer, not partial evidence. This is STRUCTURAL, not search-bounded (Axis 2 N/A). Axis 4: 11 and 809 are the object's own "leading charge primes" from the escalator/dark-hyperbola tower per docs/LITERATURE_SWEEP2_2026-07-13.md -- OBJECT-NATIVE, independent of the B706 observer-coupling seam, not a re-costumed thesis-confirmation. The verify layer (wave3_results.json) already caught two real cosmetic defects (hardcoded, non-data-branched verdict prose; a dangling "see FINDINGS below" self-citation to a file that doesn't exist -- the IKZ precondition claim is actually inherited from the pre-existing lit-sweep doc). What neither the cell nor its verify pass addresses: the headline's identification of "Redei-Reichardt 4-rank of Cl(-8899)" with "the arithmetic-CS Z/4 lift...DEGENERATES" is asserted by naming-continuity with N6's own phrasing, not derived in-cell. Checking docs/LAW_MAP.md shows this naming is partially legitimated -- B708 established at THEOREM-grade that this program's arithmetic-CS structurally IS G=Z/2 Kummer theory (H^1(G_Q,Z/2), linking=Legendre symbol = the CS linking datum), so testing whether that mod-2 datum lifts to mod-4 is an on-target continuation, not an ad hoc substitution. But that same LAW_MAP row explicitly flags "the full arithmetic-CS ACTION (Kim's bar)" as NEEDS-SPECIALIST/still open. So W3-082 computes correctly at the STRUCTURAL/mod-2-lift sub-level of a larger identification whose action-level (the loaded, physically-meaningful CS invariant) is unresolved elsewhere in the program -- "DEGENERATES" risks reading as more consequential than what was actually shown. This is a real, unflagged projection gap (analogous to trace-vs-chord blindness though not literally the same math domain), plus the finding is a single data point (one pair), not a swept law across the tower's other charge primes (809, 18845089,...). Recommend closing the bridge lemma to B708's H^1-torsor framework or reframing the headline to the fully-supported class-group claim, alongside the two verify-flagged hygiene fixes.

I re-derived every number in the cell independently (sympy resultant/factorint cross-checks on all e_0..e_4, isprime on 809 and 18845089, full factorint of e_4) and all values/factorizations in results.json/output.txt are correct. I also went adversarial beyond what the cell itself computed:

(a) Cover-side rank-of-apparition: I extended v_11(T(n))>0 checking to n=1..199 (cell only asserted the pattern from n<=12, i.e. two hits {5,10}). It holds exactly at all multiples of 5 through n=195 — this is in fact a classical Lucas-sequence rank-of-apparition fact, genuinely structural (provable for all n via the order of 11 acting on phi^2 mod 11), even though the cell only checked it empirically in a small window.

(b) Charge-side rank-of-apparition: the cell's claim "11 | e_n <=> n ≡ 1 mod 3" rested on only 2 confirmed hits (n=1,4, since NMAX_CHARGE=5 was capped by exact-integer factorization cost — e_5 is already a 65-digit product of 3 primes). This is a thin resolution for a periodicity claim. I wrote an independent mod-11 Gaussian-elimination determinant check on the escalator matrices directly (bypassing symbolic resultants/factorization entirely) and pushed it to n=10 (matrix dim 2048): zeros land exactly at n=1,4,7,10 and nowhere else — confirming the mod-3 law to double the depth the cell itself reached. No hidden positive turned up at the deeper resolution; the negative survives.

AXIS 1 (KIND) = MIXED: there's a genuine structural core (T(odd n)=L_n^2 identity; the growth-rate mismatch — cover is single-exponential phi^{2n}, charge is doubly-exponential by construction of the escalator squaring — which structurally forbids simple value-matching laws for large n) bolted onto a SEARCH-BOUNDED battery (equality/square/power/divisibility/shared-prime/index-pattern hunts over n<=12 cover, n<=5 charge as submitted). The headline "no law relating the towers" is stated as if general, but only the naive term-level law forms were tested; no proof rules out an exotic law (e.g. a sparse reindexing n->m(n), or a relation visible only mod some other prime/at some other level).

AXIS 2 (RESOLUTION) = MARGINAL: cover-side depth (n<=12, and my n<=199 check) is adequate. Charge-side depth as submitted (n<=5, 2 confirmed periodicity data points) was thin — the cell used exact factorization when a much cheaper modular-determinant method (which I demonstrated is tractable to at least n=10, likely much further) was available and unused. The verdict happened to hold up under my deeper recheck, so no false negative was hiding here, but the cell's own resolution was not self-evidently adequate at submission time — this looks more like a lucky escape than a hardened result, and the cheap modular technique should be folded into the cell for future comparable towers.

AXIS 3 (LEVEL/PROJECTION) = TRACE-BLIND-RISK, the most serious concern. The prereg itself labels this "trace-level," and both towers really are: cover-torsion is H_1 of an abelian (cyclic) cover — an inherently abelianized/Alexander-polynomial invariant; the charge tower is det(I-M_n), a conjugation-invariant eigenvalue functional of the escalator matrix. Neither construction touches ordered-word/chord structure. Per the program's own live thesis (the theta-odd/chord sector carries the dynamics; trace-level is blind to it, per the B766 lesson explicitly named in this program's memory), a trace-level "no law" here cannot rule out a chord-level correlation between a non-abelian/twisted refinement of the cover-torsion tower and the charge tower. This gap is not even flagged as a limitation in the cell's own verdict logic.

AXIS 4 (INPUT MECHANISM) = OBJECT-NATIVE / independent of the seam. Both towers are internally generated: the cover-torsion tower is a topological invariant of the figure-eight knot's Alexander polynomial; the charge tower is built from the program's own escalator/trace-map construction (tied to the B552-internal "base charge" e_1=11). Neither side is an imported SM/observer-side target (no Koide, no sin^2 theta_W, no mass ratio). So this negative is NOT merely the B706 observer-coupling seam re-costumed — it is a genuine internal cross-check between two of the object's own derived invariants, and its content (if any) would be object-native either way.

OVERALL: PROVISIONAL, not ROBUST and not wrong. Every fact checks out under independent adversarial recomputation (including deepening past the cell's own bound), so this is not a W4-194-style false-negative manufactured by under-resolution. But the claim is narrower than its headline: it rules out naive trace-level term-matching laws within a modest window, not "any law" in general, and it is silent on the chord-level question that the program's own thesis says matters most. Recommend: (1) fold the cheap mod-p determinant technique into the cell and push the charge-tower periodicity check materially further (I showed n=10 is easy; n=15-20 is likely reachable); (2) explicitly test a non-abelian/twisted (chord-level) analog of at least one tower before treating "no law" as settled at the level the program cares about; (3) tighten the base-rate/E20 control, which currently compares only 6 Lucas roots against 9 charge primes — too small a sample to make "base rate" a quantitative rather than qualitative claim.

Reproduced compute.py bit-for-bit (fresh run matches results.json/output.txt exactly). Confirmed independently: |SL(2,Z/8)|=384=8^3(1-1/4) (formula check), |2O|=48 with a UNIQUE involution, GL(2,3)!=2O (13 vs 1 involutions, correctly debunks the folklore claim), and q(q^2-1)=48 has no prime-power solution (monotonic, so exhaustive despite the code only scanning q<=11). These pieces are genuinely STRUCTURAL and the object-native alphabet <R,L> is untouched by the B706 seam (pure group/word arithmetic, not an imported observable) -- Axis 4 is clean, this is a real content-bearing test, not the seam re-costumed.

BUT the cell's central "2O is UNREACHABLE as any natural quotient" claim rests on checking exactly ONE candidate: the principal congruence quotient SL(2,Z/8)->SL(2,Z/4) (order 48, found non-isomorphic via its order-histogram). It never enumerated the OTHER normal subgroups of the right order. I closed this gap myself: SL(2,Z/8) (order 384) has exactly THREE normal subgroups of order 8 (verified by exhaustive conjugacy-class-union search, which is complete for that order). Quotient by one of the other two (not the congruence one the cell tested) has order 48 and matches 2O EXACTLY on every coarse invariant I could check: identical order-histogram {1:1,2:1,3:8,4:18,6:8,8:12}, identical center (2), identical commutator-subgroup order (24), identical abelianization (Z/2), and identical conjugacy-class-size partition [1,1,6,6,6,8,8,12] -- i.e. it passed every test the cell itself used to rule OUT SL(2,Z/4), and would have looked like a live RESOLVED-A candidate under the cell's own methodology. Only an exhaustive explicit-isomorphism search (all 96x96=9216 generator-pair correspondences, none consistent) rules it out as a genuine non-isomorphic "near-miss" group. The cell's banked proof did none of this -- it tested one quotient out of three and got lucky that the untested near-miss, when I actually checked it, turned out non-isomorphic rather than confirming 2O.

So the DIRECTION of the verdict (silver conductor-8 hearing does not realize 2O) survives a materially more thorough adversarial check and I did not find a positive. But the in-cell resolution was inadequate as executed: it is not the exhaustive proof it is billed as (Axis 1 is MIXED, not clean STRUCTURAL -- the order-growth/no-SL(2,q) facts are exhaustive, the "no natural quotient" facts were not). Axis 2 is MARGINAL for that reason. Axis 3 is fine (full SL(2) matrix/group data used throughout, not a trace-only projection, so no B766-style chord-blindness). Recommend hardening the cell: enumerate ALL normal subgroups of the relevant index and run an explicit isomorphism/homomorphism search (order-histogram+center+commutator+class-sizes match is demonstrably NOT sufficient for isomorphism at this order, as the near-miss group shows) before re-banking RESOLVED-B as a fully earned structural negative.

WHAT WAS COMPUTED (frontier/B771_phase1_wave1/cells/W4-192/compute.py, ~15s runtime): builds an explicit octonion multiplication table (Fano-plane structure constants, verified alternative/norm-multiplicative), constructs h3(O) (27-dim exceptional Jordan algebra, verified commutative with identity), then computes der(O)=g2 and der(J)=f4 as nullspaces of explicit derivation-constraint matrices via SVD (dims 14, 52 — exactly the textbook values, with enormous singular-value gaps ~1e14-1e15, so the rank/dimension counts are numerically unambiguous despite being floating-point rather than exact-arithmetic). It then computes Stab_f4 of the nested chain h2(K)⊂h3(K)⊂h3(O) for K=C (dim 12), and as an E20 comparator control for K=R (dim 15) and K=H (dim 16) — showing the recipe does NOT single out C. Finally it computes two F4/G2-orbit tangent dimensions: the complex-structure orbit inside O (6, matching the known S^6) and the primitive-idempotent orbit inside J (16, matching the known OP^2/Cayley plane). Both exactly match well-established textbook values, which is a strong internal cross-check that the whole construction (basis, structure constants, derivation solve) is correct — this is not numerology, it independently reproduces known Lie-theory facts (stab_h3C=16 matches the literature SU(3)×SU(3)/Z3, and stabC=12 matches the literature SU(3)×SU(2)×U(1)/Z6 embedding from the Baez-Schwahn/Furey octonionic program).

AXIS 1 (KIND): STRUCTURAL. This is not a parameter sweep or PSLQ-height search; it is a one-shot exact-invariant computation (dimensions of Lie algebras, stabilizers, and orbit tangent spaces) executed via robust numerical linear algebra with gaps of ~1e14-1e15 between "zero" and "nonzero" singular values — effectively as reliable as exact arithmetic here. The RESOLVED-B conclusion ("no canonical selection") rests on the *sufficient* fact that automorphism-orbit dimensions are strictly positive (6 and 16): a positive-dimensional symmetry orbit of "equally good" choices is, by construction, incompatible with a canonical/rigid pick, so this part of the argument is genuinely dispositive, not merely necessary-not-sufficient (contrast the B525 lesson). One caveat: "Stab_f4(nested C)=12" is glossed as "= dim su3⊕su2⊕u1" on dimension-count alone — the code never verifies the actual bracket/root-system structure of that 12-dim subalgebra, so the *isomorphism-type* claim ("the door is literally su3⊕su2⊕u1") is asserted, not independently checked in-cell (though it matches known literature). This doesn't undermine the RESOLVED-B verdict itself, since non-canonicity is carried entirely by the orbit-dimension facts, not by the isomorphism-type identification.

AXIS 2 (RESOLUTION): N/A — not search-bounded; the SVD gaps (14-15 orders of magnitude) leave no room for a "just beyond the bound" concern.

AXIS 3 (LEVEL): N/A in the trace/chord sense — this is a Jordan-algebra/Lie-theory computation, not an SL(n) trace-map computation, so the trace-cyclic-invariance blindness described in the brief doesn't literally apply. There IS an analogous single-level concern worth flagging: the "no canonical selection" claim is proven only relative to the automorphism group of the *pure* algebraic structure (F4/G2 acting on O, J). It does not test whether other layers of the broader program's object (arithmetic ℚ(√−3) atom, trace-map/theta-odd structure established elsewhere) could break this degeneracy and pick a preferred point invisible to pure Lie-algebra automorphisms. The cell is explicit that it stays inside this one algebraic level ("frames every observable as a property of the Jordan algebra... NO physics reading"), so the scoping is honest in the code, but the banked one-line headline ("no canonical selection of nested pair") doesn't carry that scope qualifier forward and could be over-read as a claim about the full object rather than about F4/G2-symmetry alone.

AXIS 4 (INPUT MECHANISM): MIXED, leaning imported. The computational machinery (structure constants, nullspace solves, orbit tangents) is entirely object-native — nothing is cited or imported numerically. But the *target being probed* — "does F4 canonically select the 12-dim su3⊕su2⊕u1-shaped nested pair" — has no motivation independent of the fact that su(3)⊕su(2)⊕u(1) is the Standard Model gauge algebra dimension. This is explicitly the "Baez-Schwahn" octonionic-GUT program's target, i.e. an SM-shaped object being fed in as the question to ask, not a target the Jordan-algebra structure itself would have singled out for inspection. The docstring itself frames this as "the fifth F4 door," continuing "same disease as the four closed F4 routes" — i.e. this negative was explicitly predicted in advance to fail the same way the prior four did, and the results.json/cell metadata label it "expected negative, specialness E20." By the program's own B706 seam logic (values live in the observer-object coupling, not the object), a negative that shows an SM-shaped algebraic target is NOT canonically forced by pure symmetry is exactly what the thesis already predicts — and matches a now-established pattern (4 prior closed F4 doors + this one = 5/5).

OVERALL: the arithmetic/linear-algebra is executed correctly and cross-validates against multiple independent known facts (g2=14, f4=52, S^6=6, OP^2=16, SU3×SU3=16) — this is genuinely solid, non-numerological computation, not a rubber-stamp risk on the math itself. But as a *discriminating fact for the program's thesis*, it is low-marginal-information: it re-confirms (a fifth time) that pure F4/octonion symmetry does not canonically select an SM-shaped substructure, which the thesis (and the cell's own framing as "same disease as the four closed routes") already predicted going in. This is the "seam re-confirmed in another costume" pattern, not an independent test that could have falsified the thesis in a new way (the one way it *could* have surprised — RESOLVED-A, a rigid canonical door — was checked for and correctly ruled out, so credit is due for genuinely leaving that door open before closing it). Recommend keeping RESOLVED-B as computed (no recompute needed), but (1) tag the banked headline with its actual scope ("no canonical selection under F4/G2-automorphism alone — not tested against the object's arithmetic/trace-level structure"), and (2) if the su3⊕su2⊕u1 isomorphism-type identification is ever load-bearing elsewhere, verify it via bracket/root-system computation rather than dimension count alone.

W4-304 is an independent re-verification (fresh prime seed 5e9 vs. the original 1e9) of a previously banked chain B372→B373→B387 (all committed 2026-07-03). It reproduces the N=15 gate exactly, reproduces N=45's 144/720 nonzero pair cells with zero identification failures, and finds rows a∈{6,54} identically zero, matching the prior claim independently (not a re-read of the banked JSON). The pipeline hygiene is genuinely good: exact CRT/F_p rational reconstruction across 3 primes ≡1 mod 720, held-out-embedding verification, a comparator-nonzero control row (a=1), and a documented vacuity self-test row (a=7).

AXIS 1 (KIND) — MIXED. The literal fact "const(45)=0 at rows {6,54}" is an exact deterministic algebraic identification (STRUCTURAL), not a truncated numeric search. But the arc's actual deliverable — "the seam leaves the value sector above the base level," "decoupling is a two-rung fact" — extrapolates from exactly 2 further rungs (45, 135) of an unbounded level tower; B387's own FINDINGS.md hedges this correctly ("graduates to a two-rung fact"). Scoped narrowly to level 45, W4-304's headline is fine; the surrounding narrative is SEARCH-BOUNDED/provisional and should not be over-read as a law.

AXIS 2 (RESOLUTION) — MARGINAL, for a reason the cell itself doesn't surface. I pulled sweep45.json and computed row density directly: of 60 rows in the level-45 pair table, only 24 are nonzero at all (each with exactly 6/12 nonzero cells) — 36/60 = 60% of rows are IDENTICALLY ZERO. Row 7 (the cell's own "vacuity self-test" row) is itself one of the 36 zero rows, so it returned 0/12 nonzero and added no discriminating information (the code's own comment anticipates this — "does not have to be nonzero" — so it isn't a bug, just a weak test). Given a 60% background zero-rate, "the target rows are zero" is unsurprising a priori and the "Par-dark"/"decoupling" framing claims more significance than the raw base rate supports. I checked whether the target rows {6,54} were cherry-picked from this already-visible density pattern: they were not — git history shows B373 (05:05–05:18, same day) independently derived exponent ±6 from the Weil-image-invariant single-particle sector (an unrelated structural argument about which mult-1 opposite pair is dihedral-invariant), roughly 12 hours before B387's prereg (16:55) reused that exponent as the pair-sector target. So the selection is principled, not p-hacked — but the interpretive strength of "zero" as a discovery still needs a base-rate correction the arc never performs.

AXIS 3 (LEVEL/PROJECTION) — the most substantive finding. The entire pipeline (fp_engine.py `par_trace`, `pair_cell_table` = "C[j][l] = tr(Par W1^j W2^l)") is explicitly trace-level: a cyclic-invariant twisted-trace DFT of the Weil representation. Per this audit's own framework (B766 theta-T6 lesson), traces are blind to the chord/theta-odd sector, which the program elsewhere claims is where the live structure sits. B372/B373/B387 all predate B766 and never engage the trace/chord distinction anywhere in their directories (grep confirms zero hits for "theta-odd"/"chord" in those three cell dirs). W4-304 inherits this blind spot unmodified — it re-verifies the trace-level zero with a new seed but never asks whether a chord-level (non-cyclic) construction at the same exponents would show nonzero content. This is exactly the kind of gap the audit framework flags as potentially hiding a positive.

AXIS 4 (INPUT MECHANISM) — clean. The whole construction (Weil matrices, par-trace, DFT, golden-ratio units R2, Pisano-period ratio R3, level-ratio family R4) is program-internal; the file states "No SM comparison anywhere" and this holds under inspection — no Koide/mixing-angle/mass-ratio import anywhere in the candidate set. This negative is independent of the B706 seam thesis (it isn't testing an imported observer-side target, so it can't be dismissed as thesis-predicted/content-free).

VERDICT: NEEDS-DEEPER-LEVEL. The re-verification is honest and technically sound at the level it operates on (exact zero, correctly and independently reproduced, principled target selection), but it never leaves trace-level, and the program's own stated thesis is that the live structure is theta-odd/chord. Recommend: (1) compute the analogous chord-level (non-cyclic-trace) construction at level 45 for exponents {6,54} before treating "Par-dark at 45" as closing anything; (2) extend the level-family test past 2 rungs (45, 135) before calling the decoupling a "law"; (3) if the arc is re-stated, replace "the sector is empty" framing with an explicit base-rate note (60% of rows are zero at level 45 regardless of exponent), since as currently worded it reads as more surprising than the data supports.

First-order finding: W4-139 is mislabeled as a "negative." Reading results.json/compute.py/output.txt in full and re-running compute.py from scratch (byte-identical reproduction: same disc3, same A_3(M,L) degrees 24-in-L/40-in-M, same 10/10 seed matches, same negative-control failure) confirms the cell itself reports verdict="UNRESOLVED", not a refutation. Counting it in a negatives ledger overstates its epistemic status; it should be tracked as OPEN-with-partial-progress.

AXIS 1 (KIND) — MIXED. What IS delivered is genuinely STRUCTURAL, not search-bounded: A_3(M,L) is computed by exact symbolic elimination (sympy resultants over Q[x,z,S,Psq,M,L]), verified irreducible via factor_list, and the degree facts (24 in L, 40 in M) are exact integers, not numerical fits. The construction is cross-validated by two genuinely independent code paths — an exact symbolic 3x3-minors/Cramer null-space construction (compute.py) vs an independent numeric SVD null-space solver (apoly_m3.py, a different algorithm, not a copy) — matching to <1e-3 abs / <1e-6 monodromy residual at 10 seeds across 4 x-values, plus a negative-control row selection that genuinely fails (|Ptr|,|Dettr| ~1e-27, not vacuously). This is solid methodology (Lesson B525-aware: necessary vs sufficient is explicitly respected — the cell states the degree-2-in-L reader is DISPROVED by direct computation, not assumed). But the actual target — the m=3 spectral genus, i.e. the number that would complete "3, 1, ?" — is NOT computed at all; only a trivial lower bound (genus≥1) is proven via a correctly-applied but weak Riemann–Hurwitz argument (any nonconstant map to a genus-1 curve forces genus≥1 — true for any degree≥1 cover, so it says almost nothing).

AXIS 2 (RESOLUTION) — MARGINAL/inadequate for the real question. Given A_3(M,L) has degree 24 in L and 40 in M (vs. degree 2 in L at m=1,2, where the actual genera were 3 and 1), the plane-curve genus bound (deg−1)(deg−1)≈897 dwarfs the proven floor of 1 by orders of magnitude; nothing here rules out the true m=3 genus being far larger than 1, or "resolving" to anything at all. So while the narrow disproof (reader-doesn't-transfer) is adequately resolved, the headline quantity the cell was chasing is essentially unconstrained — "≥1" cannot discriminate whether a "3,1,X" sequence carries any structural meaning.

AXIS 3 (LEVEL) — TRACE-BLIND-BUT-OK, not a risk here. The whole computation lives in SL(2,C) character-variety trace coordinates (x=tr a, z=tr(ab), S=tr(t)+1/tr(t), Psq=tr(t)^2) — but this is the standard, correct, and only sensible coordinate system for an A-polynomial/character-variety genus question (traces ARE the primitive object for SL(2,C) character varieties; there is no chord/theta-odd analogue to miss here). The cell explicitly states "no physics reading... nothing to CLAIMS.md" — it is a continuation of the B67/B89 pure low-dim-topology A-polynomial tower, orthogonal to the theta-odd/chord-level physics axis flagged by B766. So the B766-style concern (trace-blindness hiding a chord-level positive) does not apply to this cell's subject matter.

AXIS 4 (INPUT MECHANISM) — OBJECT-NATIVE, independent of the B706 seam. The quantity pursued (spectral-curve genus of the metallic-word trace-map tower's A-polynomial) is entirely self-generated by the object (the metallic tower phi_m and its fixed-locus character variety) — it references no SM/observer-side target (no Koide, no mixing angle, no coupling). A null or incomplete result here carries no thesis-predicted content-free status; it is a real open problem in the program's own generated mathematics, not a re-confirmation of the observer-coupling seam in another costume.

OVERALL — PROVISIONAL, not ROBUST and not CONTENT-FREE. The part that is finished (A_3(M,L) exact, irreducible, NOT degree-2-in-L, so the m=1/m=2 hyperelliptic reader is disproved by direct computation) is robust and reproducible. But the item as a whole should not be banked as a completed negative: the actual target (the genus number) remains open, the proven lower bound is nearly vacuous relative to the observed covering degrees, and the cell itself names an exact, well-posed, unfinished next step (fiber-product ramification/monodromy-coincidence check on the genus-1 base curve C). Recommend either (a) completing that residual computation — it is concrete and tractable with the same exact-symbolic toolchain already built and validated in this cell — or (b) at minimum re-tagging this item's ledger status from "negative" to "open/partial," since no non-existence or no-correlation claim was actually established.

synth.over_determination_finding: SHARPEST SYSTEMIC FINDING: the corpus is NOT over-determined by the seam at the level of WHAT is tested. 12/14 negatives are OBJECT-NATIVE with independent_of_seam=true — genuine internal probes (metallic traces, class-group 4-ranks, invariant trace fields, character-variety A-polynomials, commensurator indices, cyclotomic tower measures, Habiro q-series, Weil twisted-trace DFTs, escalator/cover-torsion towers) that could each have surprised the B706 thesis. Only 2/14 are seam re-costumes: W4-192 (the 5th-of-5 F4/SM-shaped door, explicitly CONTENT-FREE-THESIS-PREDICTED) and OI-055 (object-native math, but its NARRATIVE payload self-flags as echoing B706). So "one fact re-confirmed many times" is FALSE as an input-axis characterization — the over-determination is NOT the seam. The real, more dangerous over-determination is on the LEVEL/PROJECTION axis: 6/14 negatives live at trace/character/abelianized projection, and 4 of those (W3-067, W3-082, W4-115, W4-304) are flagged TRACE-BLIND-RISK against the program's OWN B766 theta-odd lesson. These conclude "no law / negative" inside a cyclic-trace or abelianized projection that by construction cannot see the chord / theta-odd / ordered-word sector the thesis itself names as where the live structure sits. The corpus therefore does not over-confirm the seam; it systematically UNDER-tests the object — at precisely the one level the object is claimed to be dead — and then risks reading trace-level absence as structural absence. Compounded on the RESOLUTION axis: 8/14 MARGINAL, driven by the recurring B525 failure mode (finite depth read as all-n in OI-055/W4-115; one candidate-of-many read as exhaustive in W4-124; external theorem cited by title not pinned in OI-055; a vacuous/tautological in-cell certificate in OI-146) — so several verdicts survived only because an adversarial recheck happened not to find the hidden positive (W4-124 and W4-115 were explicit lucky escapes, not closed proofs). NET: only 4/14 reach ROBUST. GENUINELY INDEPENDENT INFORMATION ABOUT THE OBJECT = 12/14 in principle (object-native, non-seam), but only 4/14 currently LOAD-BEARING because robust: OI-173, OI-186, W2-148r, W2-237 (all exact/structural, object-native, seam-independent, with adequate or N/A-structural resolution). The middle 8 are real object-native probes that are under-resolved or trace-blind — not seam duplicates, but not yet trustworthy negatives either.
recompute_list: W3-082 — DEEPER-LEVEL + CHORD: verdict NEEDS-DEEPER-LEVEL. Computes correctly at the mod-2 (H^1(G_Q,Z/2) Kummer/linking) sub-level, but the headline 'arithmetic-CS Z/4 lift DEGENERATES' is asserted by naming-continuity, not derived; close the bridge lemma to B708's H^1-torsor ACTION level (the loaded CS invariant, flagged NEEDS-SPECIALIST in LAW_MAP) or reframe to the fully-supported class-group claim; also single data point (11,809), not swept across the tower's other charge primes.

W4-304 — CHORD (theta-odd) PROJECTION: verdict NEEDS-DEEPER-LEVEL. Entire pipeline is trace-level (par_trace twisted-trace DFT of the Weil rep), B766-blind. Recompute the non-cyclic/chord-level analog at level 45 for exponents {6,54} before treating 'Par-dark' as closing anything; extend the level-family past 2 rungs (45,135); apply the 60%-of-rows-are-zero base-rate correction the arc never performs.

W3-067 — CHORD PROJECTION + RE-GATE: verdict PROVISIONAL, TRACE-BLIND-RISK. Classical side computed at invariant-trace-field level while the quantum comparator (B132 SU(2)_k, mod-4 T-twist) is framing/Dehn-twist (chord) data; test whether traces of the specific twist-curves entering the R/L word fuse. Also convert PSLQ bounded-height (<=1e8) to exact symbolic (Sage Groebner) trace-field certification on the 2-block cases; and the fix W4-067r is still UNTRACKED/un-re-gated in git.

W4-115 — CHORD + DEEPER OBJECT-NATIVE DEPTH: verdict PROVISIONAL, TRACE-BLIND-RISK. Both towers are trace/abelianized (H_1 of cyclic cover vs det(I-M_n)); test a non-abelian/twisted (chord-level) refinement before 'no law'. Charge-side periodicity rested on only 2 confirmed points (n<=5); fold in the cheap mod-p determinant method (shown tractable to n>=10-15) so resolution is earned not lucky.

W4-124 — OBJECT/STRUCTURAL EXHAUSTIVENESS: verdict PROVISIONAL. 'No natural quotient realizes 2O' tested exactly ONE of THREE normal subgroups of the right order; a near-miss quotient passes every coarse invariant the cell used (order-histogram, center, commutator, class-sizes) and was excluded only by explicit isomorphism search. Enumerate ALL normal subgroups of the relevant index and run explicit iso/hom search before re-banking RESOLVED-B.

W2-140 — DEEPER SEARCH SPACE: verdict PROVISIONAL. Chord-level test is fine (full n×n matrix identity, not trace-blind), but 'no closed law' covers only ~10 hand-picked forms over a 1-2-feature affine space, and the data show the tested invariant pair (e,g) is incomplete (rank n governs existence, uncombined). Widen jointly in (e,g,n), test non-affine forms / a correction term on C7 (which already fits 13/14) before treating 'no law' as durable.

OI-055 — OBJECT-NATIVE INPUT / EXTERNAL PIN: verdict PROVISIONAL, input MIXED. The load-bearing premise (Psi=Phi(h)Phi(-h) is 3-integral at EVERY order) is verified only to depth 20-100 and closed by citing GSWZ arXiv:2412.04241 by TITLE, with no pinned theorem for THIS series' Habiro-membership (contrast Thm 9.1 pinned for the companion Gevrey fact). Pin the exact statement or downgrade census label RESOLVED-B->EXTERNAL to RESOLVED-B->SEARCH-BOUNDED(depth 100).

OI-146 — RESOLUTION / REPLACE VACUOUS CERT: verdict PROVISIONAL. Headline (index 10 vs claimed 3) is sound, but PART 1a's 'decisive at M=8, no premature-plateau' certificate reuses a known-buggy nsum and is an MB12-style TAUTOLOGY (vol_m004/vol_orbifold=12 by construction regardless of the numeric value). Rerun with the Hurwitz-zeta closed form and a real SnapPy volume call (both already correct in B734/cc3_verification/b734_fix2.py) so the load-bearing premise is earned in-cell, not borrowed by luck.

W4-139 — COMPLETE OR RE-TAG (mislabeled as negative): verdict PROVISIONAL. Cell's own verdict is UNRESOLVED — no non-existence/no-correlation claim was banked, so it overstates its status in a negatives ledger. The delivered A_3(M,L) (exact, irreducible, disproves the degree-2-in-L reader) is robust, but the actual target (m=3 spectral genus, the '?' in 3,1,?) is not computed and the proven floor (genus>=1 via Riemann-Hurwitz) is near-vacuous vs covering degrees ~24/40. Complete the fiber-product ramification/monodromy computation OR re-tag ledger status to open/partial.
synth.what_we_miss: The systematic blind spot is a PROJECTION artifact, not a content shortage. The corpus tests the object almost entirely through cyclic-trace, character-theoretic, class-group, abelianized, and Lie-dimension invariants — every one of which is, by construction, invariant under the ordering/chord structure that the program's own B766 theta-odd thesis names as the carrier of the object's live dynamics. So a "no law / negative" verdict in these projections cannot distinguish "the object has no structure here" from "the structure sits one level down, in a sector this projection literally cannot resolve." Four negatives are explicitly flagged with this risk (W3-067, W3-082, W4-115, W4-304), and crucially NO cell in the corpus has yet computed a chord-level / non-abelian / framing-sensitive analog of its own target and found THAT also empty — the two cells that did reach chord level (W2-140, W4-124) were fine on that axis, which shows the check is decisive when done and simply absent when skipped. Until at least one trace-blind-risk negative is re-run at the theta-odd/chord level and STILL returns empty, the census cannot claim to have probed the object where the thesis says it lives; we may be accumulating absences in the one projection guaranteed to be silent and mistaking that for evidence about the whole object. Second, the resolution pattern (8/14 MARGINAL, several load-bearing premises surviving only because an adversarial recheck happened not to surface a hidden positive) means the negatives corpus is built partly on lucky escapes and title-only external citations rather than closed proofs — a fragile base for a "census of negatives" whose whole value is that each entry is actually a wall and not an under-resolved gap. The thing we are not understanding is therefore not the seam (which the object-native inputs genuinely test independently) but whether the object's claimed live sector has ever been looked at directly at all.
